FLOW.COMMENT: splitIF - system service for splitting an inbound document into equivalent child documents

SEQUENCE.COMMENT: splitIFflat - system service for splitting a flat file into equivalent child documents, one record per document - splits content1
This service will split a grouped document (stored as content1) into a series of individual documents based on an EOL delimeter
specified as parameter DelimEOL to this service. The output of this service is such that it will create and start a child transaction
for each separate document that it finds. If multiple documents (more than one) are found, then this service will force the workflow
to end, since this will then be considered the 'parent' transaction, meaning no mapping or delivery will occur. If only a single
document is found, then this service will exit normally and allow the workflow to continue, under the assumption that this
is a child transaction and that mapping and delivery must occur on this single document.
Note that this service will not recognize headers or footers. In general, this is designed for CSV files.
6/27/25 - Now supports both a header record and a multi-line footer

INVOKE.COMMENT: DelimEOL ==> delimEOL OPT_DELIM_FOOTER ==> delimFooter OPT_HEADER_LINE ==> headerParm

Kiwi Security Audit Point – TELNET is unsecure and we still have clients and devices talking over the network on TELNET. The preferred method is SecureSHell (SSH). This ticket is to help correct clients that are still talking over TELNET. This ticket is associated to the Count and reimage the Roll Stock Hand Scanners ticket. Roll Stock Hand Scanner Reconfiguration - RS PPM# 56338 - Assist with reconfiguring the RF Handhelds. Project Manager Name: ANDREW CLARK -- Number: 9014193815 Project Name: Roll Stock Hand Scanner Reconfiguration Project Scope: As an audit finding during the Kiwi internal audit, it was determined that using the unencrypted transmission of TELNET does not meet security standards. In order to meet security requirements, this project will move to change the transmission median from TELNET to SSH communication protocol. Sites Affected: Any sites using Kiwi. Affected Businesses: NORTH AMERICAN CONTAINER, Type of Regional Support Resource Requested: On-site LAN Admin to perform re-image of Roll Stock Hand Scanners. Cost Center: 760160 RPM #: 56115 Comments: This is a TTO project currently. We are hoping to utilize time that the LAN Admin will be on-site for already to perform these additional duties. If one-off travel is required, we will have to move this project into a PDPLite.
Scope of work: Uninstall and reinstall TTWin on computers that are identified below. Ensure that new deployments of TTWin are done through SCCM. Talk with users about prohibiting the sharing their logins.
Details: Instruct the user that you need to uninstall and reinstall TTWin. Remote into computer, delete any shortcuts on the desktop that link to TTWin or Kiwi. Go to Software Center and choose the TTWIN4 config KIWI SSH “Special K” package to install. Refer to the Project SharePoint for more information: http://spts01.ipaper.com/sites/ctait/Kiwiplan/Forms/Standard%20view.aspx?RootFolder=%2Fsites%2Fctait%2FKiwiplan%2FProjects%2F56115%20%2D%20Roll%20Stock%20Scanner%20Reconfig&InitialTabId=Ribbon%2EDocument&VisibilityContext=WSSTabPersistence
Here is a list of users and computers that are still talking on TELNET for your site:
No computers listed.

","Guadalajara, Mexico (AD6) - MSI-2 - Mexico Server Refresh Project RS-PPM# 48123 - Assist the project by uninstalling and disposing of this old server which has been decommissioned by the server group: Server Name: SAD6VI01N01 Model: ProLiant DL385p Gen8 Serial Number: USE334CTE8 Aim to have it done anytime before this Target Date: [assigned by RS-Program team]
*** New and Important***: Do not close this ticket until the Arrow pick up has taken place!! Double-check the serial number on the Arrow form. You may make arrangements with the site for one of them to be Arrow's contact. Follow up to make sure the pick up happens. Get a digitized copy of the form that the carrier for Arrow signs from the site person who handled the pick up. The serial# should be readable. Attach that form to this ticket as proof that the server was properly disposed. Then the ticket can be Closed.
* Please, properly dispose of this server on your next trip to the site. Since it has been decommissioned, you will probably find the server powered down with the disk array wiped. Having this ticket means that it is okay to shut it down, remove it, even as soon as you wish. Make sure that you have the correct old server. (The new server may be using the Name and IP address of the old server.) If you have any doubts, contact the Server Analyst for the site or contact Marshall Ammons at 901-419-5240 to make sure.
* Disconnect the server, if a rack-mount type, then remove it from the rack and store it in a place where Arrow can come to collect it (consult with the site contact to determine that place). Make every attempt to include it with at least 10 other items to avoid the $200 fee. It might take you more time than the target date, if so, let Tim Scholten know.
* Fill out the Arrow form for Disposal and send the request to Arrow via email (clientexperience@arrow.com for now). Use 362210 for the cost center if you need one. That is MSI-2's cost center and would only be used for the servers that it decommissions.
Here is the link to the PC Process Guide page that has the ""Disposal pickup form"" and instructions about the form: http://pcprocessguide.ipaper.com/northamerica/usrsrd1.asp
Here is a link to the Equipment Disposal Process document: http://spts01.ipaper.com/sites/itrs/StandardOperatingProcedures/Hardware/Equipment%20Disposal%20Process.docx Note that both tabs in the form need to be filled out for a server, Freight Estimation and Reconcilliation Detail.
* Make arrangements with the site for one of them to be Arrow's contact.
* The task will be marked complete when the RS-Program Analyst (Tim Scholten now) learns that the ticket is closed. Therefore send an email to him when you close it to get it marked done as soon as possible.
* Reminder to update your TCP/IP documents.
* BACKUP TAPES - You will need to ship the backup tapes (if there are any) to Memphis according to the instructions in the link below. Go into the document link below and then into the link to Backup Tape Shipping. https://ppm.ipaper.com/itg/servlet/Document?ID=37853&viewOnly=TRUE
Here is a link to RS Implementation documents for the MSI-2 project: https://ppm.ipaper.com/itg/project/ViewProject.do?projectId=47481&projectView=projectReferences&IS_WINDOID=N

[4/24/18, sooner if possible] - Please complete the survey and email it to Tim Scholten, and to Pablo Castillo and also post your server-related pictures to the site's location in CMDB by the end of that day (or email them to Tim Scholten if necessary).
***The MS Excel survey document and the CMDB Upload instructions are attached to this ticket.***
One of the fields on the survey asks for pictures of the servers, server racks, and network racks, to be stored in the CMDB document for each site (not in PPM). Be sure to name the files so others can know what picture is inside. e.g. Server Rack 1 Front, or Server Rm Open Area, etc.
PPM Reference Documents page for this project: https://ppm.ipaper.com/itg/project/ViewProject.do?projectId=47481&projectView=projectReferences&IS_WINDOID=N
IMPORTANT: To get a copy of any of the reference documents use the following directions: 1. Go to the Reference tab (link above) and find the file that you need. 2. Click on the blue link for that document (00_Survey__MSI-2...) 3. Click the down-arrow beside ""Save"" and choose Save As, add the site's name to the file name. 4. Save the file to a location under your control - your PC, your home drive, etc. 5. If you want to edit your copy, open it and make your changes to that file in your location and save it again. 6. Email the file to Tim Scholten (tim.scholten@ipaper.com) who will update the copy in PPM. 7. DO NOT email the pictures to Tim, but see the instructions below.

This project will research, identify, and deploy an ERP system for the Recycling business, which will replace their current ERP system, OEI/MRS for all 16 U.S. recycling facilities. We will be requested to assist MLS on wireless survey, including our own site survey, support implementation and go live activies.
The project is still in the early stages and we are currently being asked to help assist MLS in wireless site survey tasks. Regional Support will assist on MLS site tasks that includes:
1. Coordinate any required Safety training for people coming on site for this project. 2. Schedule Survey: RS to assist MLS on scheduling WiFi survey as well as Wiring installation and outages. Regional will need to work with the site contact and wireless vendor to coordinate a date and time to perform wireless survey. Review if a forklift will be needed and who will be providing the lift. 3. Complete Survey (site visit): RS to be onsite to assist the vendor for completing the WiFi survey and to complete a markup drawing showing location of scales, the number of network drops required for each scale as well as any other location(s) that may need a wired drop as part of the project). 4. Coordinate visit from bidding vendors (site visit): RS to be onsite to assist (3) bidding vendors for completing a site walk-through. 5. Coordinate Wiring (site visit): RS is required to be onsite to assists wiring vendor if needed. 6. Test WiFi: RS to be onsite to test Wifi, adjust configuration 7. RS to assist MLS for Final Documentation (Updated floorplans, new logical design, fiber layout, rack drawings or pics, tcp/ip doc, fiber spreadsheet), business/PM project status meetings, and added business scope changes.
All wireless site survey will need to be completed by October 31, 2018. MLS will work with the vendor on dates. Once they have those dates they will be reaching out to Georgetta Jackson and I will update your wireless site survey due date in this ticket.
If you have any questions, call Georgetta Jackson (513.248.6942)",10150386 GEORGETTA JACKSON,GJACKSO,KERI ENGLAND,KJOHN31,KENT RECYCLING,965,OTHER.PROJECT.CONTAINER-AMERICAS,Open,4,CHRISTOPHER BALLEW,CBALLEW,CYPRESS,4,zProject,784700,Business Project,49163,52503,9559105,Kent Rcycling (965) Mosaic Project - RS PPM# 52503 - MLS\Wireless Installation Ticket. ,"Project Name: Mosaic Project - Recycling ERP Replacement - RS PPM# 52503 RS Requirements: Regional Support has been requested to help assist with IT infrastructure on the Mosaic Project - Recycling ERP Replacement - RS PPM# 52503. Values.value: Code,Description,Customer,Item Family,Item class,Vendor,Eaches Per Case,Cases Per Pallet,Cost Per Unit,Reject Rate,Item Category,Item Type,Default Unit Of Measure,Lot Code Policy,Lot Code Rule,Expiry Date Policy,Expiry Date Rule,Expiry Date Format,Alternate Code 1,Alternate Code 2,Active,Weight Per Case,Weight Per Pallet,Personnel,Standard Units Per Hour,Performance,Track Pallets,Track Lot Code By,Quick Consume,Export To Accounting,Auto Backflush,Reserve For Projects,Auto Quarantine On Receipt,Auto Quarantine On Production,NMFC Code,Freight Class,Stop ship limit,Pick strategy,Pick strategy source,Require Physical Count during reconciliation,Reconciliation Threshold Percentage,Material Cost Type,User Code 0199005,HALF PALLET,60225,,,,1,1,9.8,,,,eaches,do not track,Lot Code Policy,do not track,Expiry Date Policy,,,,,0.001,0.001,0,0,1,true,job,false,false,,false,,false,29250 LTL/29940 TL,70,,None,Specified on Item,true,,,

INVOKE.COMMENT: remove one trailing EOL from fwcontentstring if its there

BRANCH.COMMENT: if a footer delimiter was provided, create a footer from fwcontentstring and also remove it from fwcontentstring

INVOKE.COMMENT: split fwcontentstring into a new fwcontentstring and a footer
now split fwcontentstring on delimEOL ==> docList, numDocs

BRANCH.COMMENT: decide from headerParm how to handle a potential header row

SEQUENCE.NAME: headerParm=='KEEP'
SEQUENCE.COMMENT: if we have a header and are keeping it, we need to append the EOL to it

INVOKE.COMMENT: in delimEOL fix it with true CR
in delimEOL fix it with true LF

SEQUENCE.NAME: headerParm=='REMOVE'
SEQUENCE.COMMENT: if we are removing it, we just set header to blank

SEQUENCE.NAME: headerParm=='NONE'
SEQUENCE.COMMENT: if there is no header, just set header to blank

BRANCH.COMMENT: now depending on whether we have 1 doc or many docs, we fall through and do nothing, or split them and create children

SEQUENCE.NAME: numDocs>1
SEQUENCE.COMMENT: we have more than one document, so we have to fix up all documents with a new header and footer

BRANCH.COMMENT: if we are not SKIPing the header row, then create a child for this record

SEQUENCE.NAME: CHILD
SEQUENCE.COMMENT: need to capture any errors in creating/starting the CHILD transaction, so that we can continue

record.value: 0 or the sessionid of the caller
if parent is not 0, this is the child number starting with 1

INVOKE.COMMENT: move the current workflow (the parent) to its last step, so that the parent can end quietly

SEQUENCE.NAME: $default
SEQUENCE.COMMENT: we have only a single document, so do nothing

INVOKE.COMMENT: content1 did not split - workflow will continue

Values.value: content1 did not split - workflow will continue
